entry.title: Which sales doc type & item category should I use for order related billing in S/4 HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am currently working with SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition. My client has a requirement for an order-related billing process within this environment.

However, I have encountered an issue as there are no standard sales document types and item categories available in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition , to support the required process

I am reaching out for your expert guidance on this matter.

Could you please assist me in configuring an "Order-related Billing Process" within the SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud?

Your expertise would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance

Hi @shailesh_mishra5 

Can you please share what EWM settings required for subassembly in this scenario. I tried this process but not working. No goods movement posting happening.

entry.title: Re: Fiori Elements: Value Help hidden
entry.content: HI, If you are using CDS, we have possibility to remove that field and hide, or if you are using smart table/filter bar, there from control configuration we can hide that field.

Perhaps the API reference is what you are looking for. 

API Reference - Demo Kit - SAPUI5 SDK

entry.title: Re: Configuring Standard API in S/4 HANA Cloud
entry.content: Good One, Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Minimum required SAP Kernel Patchlevel for SLES 15 (S...
entry.content: I know that SAP releases a kernel for Linux and not especially for SLES - but since SLES versions and patchlevels differ from other Linux distributions, there will certainly be different Kernel patchlevel prerequisites for SLES than for RHEL or ORACLE Linux. In the kernel release notes you see e.g. the minimum required OS level - what I want to find out is the minimum Kernel Patchlevel for a certain OS level.

entry.title: Beyond Automation: The Strategic Role of AI in Boosting Business Productivity
entry.content:
2023 was the year of Artificial Intelligence (AI). From the historic popularity of ChatGPT to the huge investments in making organizations AI-driven, AI has revolutionized the entire tech space. Today, it has become a non-negotiable business imperative to thrive in the speed-first age of the digital world. Even, reports revealed that the global AI market is projected to rise by 36.8% from 2023 to 2030 at CAGR. 

Now, it’s time to shift the conversation from AI as a futuristic concept to AI as a productivity multiplier. As per my vantage point, AI’s true potential lies not in blindly following the hype but in its right practical implementation. Here the question isn't just about adopting AI, but about strategically integrating it to achieve a 3x boost in business performance. 

We all are in the thick of a workplace revolution where most businesses are impulsively investing in AI to stay ahead of the trend. Let me clear here, the victory won't go to those who simply invest the most. It will be claimed by those who can effectively harness AI to drive tangible results. This means moving beyond buzzwords and focusing on real-world solutions that deliver measurable value. 

Even a study found that organizations with mature AI integration drives 3x increase in productivity. Having led Radixweb for more than two decades, I have witnessed the transformative potential of AI when implemented strategically. I strongly believe, it’s not just about technology; it’s about understanding your business vision, your people, the changing need of your processes and aligning AI to empower your strengths.  

In this article, I’ll share my insights on how to build a robust AI strategy and execute it effectively to achieve tangible results in terms of business productivity. But before that, let’s navigate the challenges that come with AI integration. Let’s get started!  

Navigating the Challenges for Driving Real Benefits of AI 

While there's undeniable excitement and momentum around AI, let's be honest – it's not all sunshine and rainbows. There are wide array of hurdles to overcome. I have observed that many organizations are dipping their toes in the artificial Intelligence services , but only few are truly diving in meaningfully. Here’s why:  

  • AI Illiteracy: Unfamiliarity with AI is one of the biggest challenges. Currently, only two-thirds (64%) of the workforce have little to no familiarity with the GenAI tools they could embrace in their work. Remember, until your workforce is not skilled to harness AI’s potential, your firm will continue struggling to capitalizing on its real benefits. 
  • Data Distrust: While using GenAI at work, data distrust is the core concern. Because AI is only as good as the data it's fed. That’s where, many of us worry about making crucial decisions using unreliable information from GenAI. In addition, there is a risk of compromising an organization’s data privacy besides delivering biased results.  
  • AI Uncertainty: Uncertainty about AI's role within the organization is another obstacle. Majority of people are wonder, "Will AI replace my job?" Lack of clarity has also arisen concerns in the workforce about the integration and use cases of AI in business processes. These concerns take birth from the perception that leveraging AI will render an employee lazy, shrink responsibilities, whereas it would rather be a legitimate source for empowering productivity. 
  • Ad-hoc AI adoption: Often, AI adoption is ad hoc. Most of the teams are experimenting with AI without a cohesive strategy. This leads to inefficiencies and missed opportunities. Studies shown that only 12% of firms have mature AI integration strategies, revealing a huge gap in strategic planning. 

Proven Strategies For AI Integration That Drive Real Results  

Let’s talk tactics. We have discovered the challenges; now, it’s time to implement solutions. Here are some proven strategies that helped me drive tangible business outcomes with AI integration:  

  • Cultivate an AI-Literate Workforce  

Let's talk about the people side of AI. In today's digital age, everyone, from the C-suite to the frontline, needs a basic understanding of AI. It's no longer optional; it's a necessity. I've been surprised by the number of employees independently exploring AI tools. This grassroots enthusiasm is good, but it also highlights the need for structured learning. It's about creating a culture where everyone feels comfortable discussing and experimenting with AI. We need to shift from an 'AI expert' mindset to an 'AI-enabled workforce' mentality. 

  • Prioritize Data Concerns and Quality  

Data is the fuel that powers AI engines. But poor-quality data is like using contaminated fuel that will only break down your engine. That’s why I emphasize treating data as a strategic asset, not just a byproduct of operations. We must invest in data cleaning, validation, and governance. It's not just about compliance; it's about ensuring the accuracy and reliability of our AI-driven decisions. 

  • Foster Human-AI Collaboration  

Now, it might sound cliché but true: AI is a tool, not a replacement. It is here to augment and widen human capabilities, not eliminate them. Humans bring creativity, judgement, and empathy to the table. AI widens the scope for these capabilities. We need to design systems where people and AI complement each other's strengths. By fostering a collaborative relationship between humans and AI, we can achieve breakthroughs that would be impossible for either to accomplish alone. 

  • Build Robust AI Governance  

AI is a powerful technology, and with great power comes great responsibility. We need a robust framework to ensure AI is used ethically and responsibly. This includes developing clear guidelines, following data privacy, and risk management practices. It also helps you strike the right balance between innovation and control.  

  • Calibration of AI Performance 

We can't improve what we don't measure. Therefore, AI initiatives require constant evaluation like any other business investment. We need to establish key performance indicators (KPIs) to track the impact of our AI investments. By analyzing these metrics, we can identify what's working, what's not, and make data-driven adjustments. It's about maximizing the return on our AI initiatives.  

Wrapping Up  

Undoubtedly, the journey of strategic AI integration is undoubtedly challenging. It requires a delicate balance of technology, people, and process to reap productivity and real results. However, organizations that master this complex interplay will not only survive but thrive in the digital age. 

I envision a future where AI is not just a tool, but an extension of human capability, a force multiplier that propels us forward. It's a future where work isn't solely about production, but about cracking human potential to imagine and realize new possibilities. This is the future state of AI at work. 

I ensure by following the strategies outlined above, you will be able to position your organization at the forefront of the AI revolution. So, get ready to embrace this transformation with courage and conviction.

entry.title: Re: Unable to convert an SAP Script as a PDF and send it...
entry.content: Please don't post an answer, which is reserved to propose a solution. Instead click on "Replies" of the question and then "Comment". Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Automatic Synchronization not working in SAP Service...
entry.content:
Hey you need to check below points -> 

1. whether feature CA_AUTO_SYNC is enabled or not

2. check whether this feature CA_AUTO_SYNC is enabled or not for your persona

checa la clase de tratamiento 62=9 y los valores después de la función MXSI, a lo mejor por ahí encuentras algo.

Johnatan

entry.title: Re: Talent Intelligence Hub - Myths and Misconceptions - Part 2 (Migration)
entry.content:
@DaliborPetrovic thank you for this!

Do you know if there is any reading on ability to switch competencies to skills and vice versa? Our organization is implementing this all for the first time via the Talent Intelligence Hub and I think there is a real possibility some of this may be changed.

Best,

entry.title: Re: SAP DM - EWM Goods Receipt Failed
entry.content:
Update:

we have been able to fix this issue. The Problem was, that we had to change the "NoAsgnmt" Parameter in the Following Path for our Packaging Material Type from "A SSCC" to "B Number Range Interval"

SAP Customizing Implementation Guide->SCM Extended Warehouse Management->Extended Warehouse Management->Cross-Process Settings->Handling Units->Basics->Define Packaging Material Types for Warehousing

entry.title: Re: Custom data as table, CDS, Domain, Business object and all that jazz...
entry.content:
Hello @egbertvenema_mybrand 

For Public Cloud I'm afraid that is correct. CDS views created by "custom apps" (such as Custom CDS views or Custom Business Object) are only released for Key User extensibility, not for developer extensibility, which means you cannot use them in ADT in your own development. Here is a note for this 3387003 - The use of CDS Entity XYZ is not permitted - Developer Extensibility - SAP S/4HANA Cloud

entry.title: Re: Minimum required SAP Kernel Patchlevel for SLES 15 (S...
entry.content: I do agree that's reasonable expectation however not every OS vendor defines similar style of release notes into such details. From SAP end we always recommend customers to keep pace with SAP kernel patch releases then a "minimum" required version won't serve well. A simpler example, if you are planning to move from SLES12sp5 to SLES15sp6, you may consider upgrade your PAM certified SAP kernel release to a more recent PL. If you are upgrading from simply SLES15sp3 or sp5 to sp6, no action is required. Patching SAP kernel up to date is still highly and always recommended.

entry.title: Re: Eclipse IDE Loading Enhancements for [XXX] MV45AFZZ" has encountered a problem
entry.content:
Sap Referanced this note. Also , "The Eclipse client will be fixed with the next delivery of the same. This will be version 2411."

https://me.sap.com/notes/3511352/E

entry.title: how to get OpenItem (Reference to Items) information for BankPaymentAdvice
entry.content:
Hi,

We want to know how to get OpenItem (Reference to Items) information for BankPaymentAdvice in SAP Cloud Applications Studio?

We checked the corresponding BusinessObject PaymentAdvice in the Repository Explorer and did not find the corresponding node.

Thanks!


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: how to get OpenItem (Reference to Items) information...
entry.content:
The information of the Referenced Items would be in PaymentExplanationPaymentExplanation assoication. The PaymentExplanation holds the totals and the associated items store the details of the referenced items. They can use Payment Reference/Invoice References to match the items to invoices already existing in the system. 

Open the PaymentAdvice and note the UUID of the Advice

Use the Query QueryByElements of the BO DuePayment and load the Payment Advice UUID into the field PaymentReceivingBusinessTransactionDocumentReference/UUID/content.

 

From this Due Payment instance navigate by association to DueClearingExplanationItem (one instance for each invoice in the Advice). In this node, note the ClearedTradeReceivablesPayablesRegisterItemUUID.

 

Finally, load the BO TradeReceivablesPayablesRegister, node ItemOverview. Use the query QueryByElements and use the ClearedTradeReceivablesPayablesRegisterItemUUID value in the field UUID. This should show the Item elements.

entry.title: Re: Build your app (new UI)?
entry.content: This is in progress with high priority, so should be resolved by today/tomorrow.

entry.title: HANA Cloud - Cockpit does not have 'Configure Email' button
entry.content:
Hi Support,

Our HANA Cloud cockpit does not have configure email button, thus we can't send email alert automatically. Is DBADMIN missing authorization?

In the On-Premise HANA system Cockpit, we can see the button on top-right corner.

Best Regards,


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: HANA Cloud - Cockpit does not have 'Configure Email'...
entry.content:
Hello,

This is the correct behaviour as in BTP there is a service that you can avail of for alert notifications. Please refer to the documentation on how to set up this service and further information.

What is SAP Alert Notification Service for SAP BTP? | SAP Help Portal

Alerts, Notifications and System Metrics in SAP HANA Cloud | SAP Help Portal

Thanks & Regards,

entry.title: Re: What’s New in SAP Analytics Cloud Modeling Extensions & Integration QRC Q1 2024 Release
entry.content:
Dear Amanda

In the first part of your post, you explain that "In QRC Q1 2024, the API has been further extended to also support Master Data for Public Dimensions." 

And add this includes data access control. When trying to export data acces control of a public dimension (read and write column) are not exported.

We use the following type of URL :

 /api/v1/dataexport/providers/sac_public_dimensions/t.2:Profit_Center/PublicDimensionData

Can you confirm data access controls can be export using API ?

Thanks in advance

Kind regards

Emmanuel

entry.title: Re: Unable to convert an SAP Script as a PDF and send it...
entry.content:
Got it. SCMS_BINARY_TO_XSTRING considers only the first component of parameter BINARY_TAB, in your case the lines of LT_PDF are of type TLINE which is made of 2 components TDFORMAT (2 characters) and TDLINE (132 characters), so it considers only TDFORMAT instead of both.

To convert LT_PDF to XSTRING, there must be no code page conversion to decode LT_PDF because CONVERT_OTF_2_PDF returns the PDF bytes without code page conversion into the characters of LT_PDF. There are several ways, the simplest is the below one. It's based on the feature IMPORT ... IN CHAR-TO-HEX MODE to move characters to bytes without code page conversion. Notice also that it's important to use CONCATENATE ... RESPECTING BLANKS, just in case the last 2 bytes of one line correspond to one space character.

DATA lt_pdf TYPE TABLE OF tline.
DATA lv_string TYPE string.
DATA ev_pdf_xstring TYPE xstring.
DATA lv_buffer TYPE xstring.

CONCATENATE LINES OF lt_pdf INTO lv_string RESPECTING BLANKS.

EXPORT any_name = lv_string TO DATA BUFFER lv_buffer.
IMPORT any_name TO ev_pdf_xstring FROM DATA BUFFER lv_buffer IN CHAR-TO-HEX MODE.

entry.title: Not able to create the Communication Arrangement in S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Dear Colleagues,

I came across this error when creating a Communication Arrangements SAP_COM_0008 in S4 HANA Cloud Public additional & I would like to share this information.

When saving the Communication Arrangement the following error appears:

" Multiple Addresses not supported in source system"

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Not able to create the Communication Arrangement in S...
entry.content:
Hello,

The Multiple Address Handling is not activated on this system.

When creating the Communication Arrangements in the Additional Properties "No Multiple Address Handling" the property value should be empty.

This is not required when the Multiple Address Handling is not active on the system.

Kind Regards,

Liam

entry.title: Re: Not able to edit Onetime deduction portlet
entry.content:
Hi,

You can check below KBA.

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2707908

entry.title: Build Process Automation Custom Form - Consuming API via Destination
entry.content:
Dear community,
we are creating a workflow with SAP BPA that uses a custom form. In this form we need to make POST and DEL calls consuming SAP oData.

SAP is reachable via Cloud Connector, the Destination has been created, imported into BPA, Actions have been created to test that everything is working correctly. Up to this point everything is fine.

The problem arises when we try to use these services in the custom approval form controller.

 

 

 

 

 

 

In xs-app-json we added this route

{
  "source": "^/odata/(.*)$",
  "target": "/$1",
  "destination": "sap_process_automation_odata",
  "authenticationType": "none",
  "csrfProtection": false
},

in form.controller.js we added this code

_updateOdata: function() {
  $.ajax({
    url: that._getODataURL(),
    method: "GET",
    async: false,
    contentType: "application/json",
    headers: {
      "X-CSRF-Token": "fetch",
    },
    complete: function (result, status) {
      token = result.getResponseHeader("X-CSRF-Token");
      console.log("SapToken: " + sapToken);
    },
  });
  /* OTHER CODE*/
}

_getODataURL: function () {
  var appId = this.getOwnerComponent().getManifestEntry("/sap.app/id");
  var appPath = appId.replaceAll(".", "/");
  var appModulePath = jQuery.sap.getModulePath(appPath);

  return appModulePath + "/odata/sap/ZODATA";
},

What I expect to happen with this code is to get the token to use for the next POST or DEL call, but the call fails with a 500 or 404 error.

The way the route is configured, the address should resolve to
https://<host>:44301/sap/opu/odata/sap/ZODATA
but evidently something is not correct.

I add that this form allows you to write and read files in the BTP Document Management Service via API, using a very similar approach and this is working.

I have done this in the past on other custom task forms, but with Workflow Manager (maybe there is some difference that we have not noticed).

Anyone have any suggestions?

Thanks and Best Regards,
Giampaolo

SAP Build Process Automation

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SlipStream Issue with QM02
entry.content: Hi Tamas,

entry.title: Re: Method to download Flexible workflow from FIORI(simil...
entry.content:
Hi Team, 

In the 'Manage Workflow Scenarios' App, this functionality is available:

Key Features

You can use this app to:

  • View a list of available stages for a selected workflow scenario

  • Display the stage of a process-layer workflow scenario

  • Display the details of a scenario stage

  • Export a workflow scenario

  • Import a workflow scenario

I hope this helps,

Kind Regards, 
Lisa 
SAP Support

entry.title: SAP Data Migration Cockpit (Migrate Your Data) Create Custom Object Documentation
entry.content:
Hello Guru's

I have been working with the Data Migration Cockpit / Migrate Your Data App (formerly LTMC) for a few years but have only recently created some custom objects/programs for doing UPDATES to Business Partner (BP) Address and/or E-Mail Address information, which I have got working successfully, despite being a non-developer.

I know that in the Migration Object Modeler (LTMOM) I can maintain information as to how the program is working (where the developer normally enters their comments), but I want to create a document where the users can access it from the DMC Object that describes the functionality of my custom object.

I have had a look online but I haven't found anything so far, so if this is possible I would appreciate some guidance on it.

entry.title: Re: Customer Business Partner Change Log History Query
entry.content:
Have you tried the query?

I can't remember when it came into SAPB1 but it is long time ago. Is your SAPB1-Version sooo old?

entry.title: SAP IAS User store
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

We have installed IAS as a proxy and are using Azure AD as our IdP. We plan to use SAP BAS (via BTP) and SAC.

My question is: Is it recommended that users exist in the IAS user store, meaning that users should first be provisioned to the IAS user store? Or is it preferable to provision users directly from Azure AD to a cloud application without storing them in the IAS user store first? What are the best practices for this?

Thank you for any tips.

Many thanks and best regards

entry.title: Re: Upload .XML file to CPI endpoint
entry.content:
Hello @Akash,

Yes it's possible, but I would like to understand what you exactly want. 

You can use Data Store to save your XMLs, but it's not an efficient way. And what are you going to do with your XMLs ?

Best Regards,

Burak

entry.title: SAC Report Broadcasting - Blog 2 - Live Data
entry.content:
Introduction

Hello and welcome to part 2 of this blog series. In this blog I will be discussing the functionality of report broadcasting in SAC when the data is live from a data source. I will address the following points:

  • Supported Live Connections
  • Setting up
  • How to create publications
  • View and Modify your Scheduled Publications
  • Data Validation and Refresh

 

Supported Live Connections

You can schedule publications in SAC for the following live data sources:

  • SAP BW
  • SAP HANA
  • SAP S/4HANA
  • SAP HANA 2.0
  • SAP HANA Cloud (Must be a HANA Cloud connection type)
  • Stories based on live data tunnel connections

Unfortunately, data coming live from SAP Datasphere into SAC cannot be scheduled.

 

Setting up

The first step you need to take to set up SAC report broadcasting for live connections is allowing the tenant to schedule a publication. You can set up the tenant so that SAC can publish to either SAP users or non-SAP users or both. This is the same step for scheduling import data for publication however there are some additional steps that need to be taken.

 

  1. Login to your SAC tenant
  2. Go to System >> Administration. Click on the System Configuration tab and toggle the two schedule publication options on.
  3. Go to the Data Source Configuration tab and scroll down to the ‘Live Data Sources’ section. You need  to toggle the ‘Allow live data to securely leave my network’ on.
  4. Navigate to your Connections app and select and edit your live connection. Open the ‘Advanced Features’ header. Check the box beside ‘Let users set up a schedule for publishing stories’. This will use the credentials of the user who schedules the story publishing to be used to retrieve the data.
  5. You will be asked to select a Location ID as well as a Virtual Host and Port.

6. Once these advanced settings are configured successfully, you can schedule a story created on a live connection.

 

How to create publications

  1. Go to your files in SAC
  2. Select the story or analytical application you want to schedule for publication. Choose the Share option and select Schedule Publication
  3. The Schedule Publication window will open. Here you will add your publication information such as event name, start date etc.

Name

Give your Schedule a name

Start Date

Give your schedule a start date and time. You can add a recurrence so that the publication is scheduled to recur every day/ week/ month etc. You can also add details about ending the recurrence.

File Type

You can choose between either a PDF, PowerPoint or CSV

Email Subject

This is the subject for the email which would be delivered to the recipients

Email Message

This is the message body for the email which would be sent to the recipients over email

Email Delivery Options

You can choose which email delivery option you’d like:

·       Attachment

·       Attachment and Preview Inline

·       Preview Inline

Include link to story

If you select this checkbox, the story/ analytic application link would be sent along with the email.

 

4. Add details to the Distribution heading

SAP Analytics Cloud Recipients

Add SAP users or teams to this view

Non-SAP Analytics Cloud Recipients

Add non-SAP users to this view e.g. joe.bloggs@hotmail.com . You can input a maximum of 3 non-SAC users.

Story view

You can choose the story, global bookmarks or my bookmarks.

File Name

The name the file will appear under in your email as a attachment

 

5. Click on Create. This will set up a schedule a publication.

 

View and Modify your Scheduled Publications

  1. Go to the Calendar app in SAC
  2. You can see in Calendar, your scheduled publication events and its recurrence.
  3. You can also edit you’re a recurrence event. Select the recurrence event from the calendar view and on your right side, a new panel opens where you can modify.

 

 

Data Validation and Refresh

Like our previous blog, it is important for the data in the scheduled publications are up to date an accurate especially if they are recurring. A user does not want to receive the wrong data or the same data that they received in last week’s report. Live data is live meaning it is always up to date and refreshed.

 

Conclusion

SAC Report Broadcasting with live data simplifies the work of delivering up-to-date reports to your audience. By supporting various live data connections and offering flexible scheduling options, it ensures that recipients always receive the most current information. This functionality enhances the accuracy and reliability of your reporting, making it an essential tool for real-time data distribution.

entry.title: Re: Task 3 - Enhancing CDS View Entities with Annotations
entry.content:
Please find my submission for Task-3 below

  1. Preview the App showing all the columns, search and filter options in the CDS view entity.2. Search records based on a specific value from 'Description' field and select an available value from the 'Status' field.

entry.title: My Integration Suite was deleted automatically, now it is not showing under Subscriptions
Re: Error in direct input(RCPTRA02) LSMW for Routing mass...
entry.content: You could try a break-point in FM such as CM_F_MESSAGE or CM_F_SELECT_T100.

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to enhance My Inbox app on S4 Cloud?
entry.content:
Hi,

Currently it is not supported to add custom fields in My Inbox - Basic Details screen.

Please refer to this KBA for your information:
3368892 - "Adapt UI" don't work inside My Inbox application

If it is really important to enable the mentioned functionality for your business, you may consider raising a feature request. SAP Customer Influence enables customers to submit improvement requests and to openly vote on them. 
The detailed steps are as follows:
1. Get started by opening SAP Customer Influence (https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/).
2. Register and accept the Terms of Use
3. Choose a session of your interest
4. Search whether there are existing improvement requests and if yes, start voting and commenting
5. If no existing improvement requests, create and submit your improvement request
Also you can refer to the KBA 2963059 for more details about New Feature Request in SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

Best regards,

SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Certify Your Skills as SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Implementation Consultant
entry.content:
Hello MikeBretz,

Yes, with studying the Learning Journeys listed in the “How to prepare” section plus the Learning Journey “Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition” (keep in mind that the Learning Journey “Exploring SAP Cloud ERP” https://learning.sap.com/learning-journey/explore-sap-cloud-erp is a prerequisite) you are well prepared for the certification.

In addition pls. have a look at the Topic areas (topics, items, weighting) and to deepen your knowledge we recommend practicing your skills in a live SAP environment. You find the related information under “Hands-on Practice” (these practice systems are preconfigured with the data you need to carry out exercises from the learning journey) for each Learning Journey.

I hope you enjoy your preparations and wish you every success with your certification.

Best regards, Dieter

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Code Storyboard
entry.content:
Hi @T1mey , from application logic (custom event handler) point of view, no distinguish on the draft entity or not, as both draft info and the committed entity data are shared in the same db entity table. And the corresponding app logic will get triggered when the draft entity standard events, such as READ, CREATE, etc.

Would you show more detail in which case the handler is not triggered as expected? thanks.

Regards,

Xin

entry.title: PM orders on the basis of completion of each orders.
entry.content:
Hi,

In maintenance plan, there are many orders in the call number, and all completed on different dates. User wanted to generate next orders on the basis of completion date of each order. Not on the basis of actual variance of the call number. Is there any possibility?

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori Innovation Day in Chicago: SAP Fiori fundamentals, UX transformation, and AI innovatio
entry.content: Great work and nice agenda. Would something similar also be possible for individual customers? We get quite a lot of requests that point in that direction. Whom would I need to contact here?

entry.title: Re: Upload .XML file to CPI endpoint
entry.content: The XML post to the on-premise system using odata V2 adapter

entry.title: Re: RBNK_IMPORT_PAYM_STATUS_REPORT error XSLT
entry.content:
The most common cause I have encountered is that the PSR file's <MsgId> tab might not contain the REGUT-RENUM of the associated batch, either as XXXXXXXX or as XXXXXXXX-otherText.

Secondly, there could be an issue with the BCM configuration: SPRO -> FSCM -> BCM -> Payment Status Management -> Map External/Internal Status

If both of these are correct, please check the PSR file for potential data issues.

entry.title: Re: RBNK_IMPORT_PAYM_STATUS_REPORT error XSLT
entry.content: Kindly debug the method II_BNK_CPAY_ORD_NOTIFICATION~EXECUTE_ASYNCHRONOUS of class CL_BNK_CPAY_ORD_NOTIFICATION using an external breakpoint if you need further assistance.

entry.title: Re: Transport Error: Request in "Release Started" state
entry.content:
This is some more information than necessary, but others might find it useful when trying to delete an object.

For the people who actually get the error message, when trying to delete an object that is locked because it's in a request that has the statues 'release started':

You can find the request with status 'release started' via transaction SE03.

Menu point 'Request/Tasks' -> 'Find Requests'

There you can actually select for any request that has status 'Release started'.

Once you find it, you can try to right-click on it and use 'Release directly'. Then it will try to release it again and then finish it maybe successfully, maybe with an error. But then the status is no longer 'Release started'.

This means, you can then proceed with the deletion of your object without any issues.

entry.title: Optimizing Multithreaded Data Insertion into SAP Data Lake: Seeking Guidance and Best Practices
entry.content:
I am working on a project where I need to insert data into SAP Data Lake using a multithreaded Java application. This application uses JDBC to batch-insert log entries into a specific table in the Data Lake. My goal is to optimize the performance of these batch inserts, ensuring that resources are used efficiently while minimizing errors or conflicts, such as constraint violations.

Technical Implementation:

  1. Multithreaded Design:

    • The application uses an ExecutorService to manage multiple threads that execute batch insert operations concurrently. Each thread runs a Callable task that establishes a database connection and performs the batch insert.
    • To prevent conflicts between threads during data insertion, I use table-level locking (LOCK TABLE ... IN EXCLUSIVE MODE). I also manage transactions manually to ensure that batches are either fully committed or rolled back in case of errors.

  2. Metrics and Logging:

    • I’ve implemented detailed logging to capture task execution details, including thread name, group, and processing time.
    • After all threads have completed their tasks, I execute a summary SQL query to count the number of entries inserted since the start of the current run, using a timestamp filter.
    • I also calculate statistics, such as the number of entries inserted per second by each thread, to assess individual thread performance.

  3. SQL Operations:

    • The application includes SQL statements to retrieve distinct entries based on a specific index column and to count the number of occurrences for each index.
    • I run a summary query post-execution to determine the total number of entries inserted during the current application run.

Challenges Encountered:

  • Performance Considerations: While the application performs as expected, I want to evaluate its efficiency, particularly in terms of processing time, resource utilization, and its ability to handle large-scale data inserts.

Questions for the SAP Data Lake Team:

  1. Best Practices for Locking and Concurrency:

    • Is my current approach of using LOCK TABLE ... IN EXCLUSIVE MODE for handling concurrent inserts considered best practice in SAP Data Lake? Are there more efficient methods or alternatives that you would recommend?

  2. Optimizing Batch Size and Retry Logic:

    • What batch sizes and retry mechanisms do you recommend in SAP Data Lake to enhance performance and reduce error rates?

  3. Connection Management:

    • Does the SAP Data Lake JDBC driver support connection pooling, and how should I configure it to optimize performance?

  4. Performance of SQL Queries:

    • Are there specific indexes or other optimizations I should apply to the table to improve the performance of counting and filtering operations, especially given the high volume of data being inserted?

  5. Recommended Isolation Levels:

    • Considering the use of transactions, what isolation level would you recommend to balance performance and data integrity in SAP Data Lake?

  6. Logging and Monitoring:

    • What tools or practices do you recommend for monitoring and logging the performance of batch inserts?

  7. Threading Model Optimization:

    • Do you have any advice on optimizing the threading model, possibly using SAP Data Lake-specific features or configurations, to improve the performance of the application?

Request for Guidance:

I am seeking advice on the best approach to test the performance of SAP Data Lake. I would appreciate recommendations on how to conduct effective performance testing and whether my current approach aligns with best practices. Any guidance on improving my implementation would be invaluable, especially insights into potential pitfalls and optimization strategies.

entry.title: Re: How to Use Public API Service for Condition Records - Part 4
entry.content:
Hello, I am trying to modify the value of a Purchasing Condition Record without success.

In the first step I use the GET_CONDITION_RECORD method to retrieve the data, then I modify the ConditionRateValue field, then I execute the UPDATE_CONDITION_RECORD method with the modified data (ConditionRateValue), the execution goes well - no error message - but nothing happens, the modifications are not saved. I do not see what I am doing wrong.

Does anyone have an example of code that implements an UPDATE?

and in the wizard I can only select the entity, not the draft.

entry.title: Re: What’s New in SAP Analytics Cloud Modeling Extensions & Integration QRC Q1 2024 Release
entry.content:
Hello @Emmanuel1 ,

Thanks a lot for your great feedback.  After some investigation by our team, it is confirmed that this is currently not supported, so I will update the blog to reflect this.

I shall also request that our team extend the API to include DAC for Master Data - Public Dimensions in the future and will provide an update here once this has been made available.

Thanks again for highlighting.

Best regards,

Amanda

entry.title: Re: When deploying an app in Build Apps with Cloud Foundr...
entry.content:
When you enable Cloud Foundry in your subaccount, you enable the Cloud Foundry environment. This does not mean any consumption of the Cloud Foundry Runtime yet. When you use your subaccount for Build Apps only and deploy your Build Apps applications there, you will not see Cloud Foundry Runtime consumption in the usage statistics of your global account nor your subaccount and you will not be charged with Cloud Foundry Runtime cost. Even if you assign some amount of the CF Runtime to your space, it will still show 0 use of the Cloud Foundry Runtime and all the assigned amount will be shown as available for this and other spaces.

But if you use SAP Business Application Studio to write, build and deploy your application, then you need to assign some Cloud Foundry Runtime capacity to the space where you deploy your Pro-Code application and it will be used and you will be charged for it.

entry.title: remove/hide partially delivered & TECOed purchase order from VL10B display list
entry.content:
Hello

 

1- How can i remove or hide a TECOed & partially delivered purchase order from VL10B display list ?

2- How can i change delivery note print order, i.e. want to display bin order

 

Thanks

Nabeel

entry.title: Re: How to identify the exact SQL query sent to source system?
entry.content: You should be able to check the query which is sent to remote source in Datasphere at remote query monitor in Data integration.

entry.title: Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core
entry.content:
This SAP CodeJam event is on the topic of Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core and Python. You do not need to have any background in the topic, but a lot of curiosity!

In this CodeJam you will learn how to use Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core to implement a retrieval augmented generation (RAG) use case to improve the responses of large language models (LLMs) and reduce hallucinations. You will learn how to deploy an LLM on SAP AI Core and query it via SAP AI Launchpad and the Python SDK. Furthermore, you will learn about the most important genAI concepts and create and use text chunks and embeddings to improve your RAG response.

This is a free in-person event and is planned for Friday, September 20st in Warsaw, Poland 🇵🇱 from 14:00 to 18:00 CEST (GMT+01:00). The language of the event and the content will be in English. The instructor is a Developer Advocate Nora von Thenen.

The event is being hosted by KMD Poland at
Inflancka 4a, 00-189 Warszawa, Poland

Note that spaces are limited: we can only accommodate 30 attendees.

Visit the SAP CodeJam group for more information on SAP CodeJam events, to ask questions, and to chat with your fellow attendees.

entry.title: SD: Use price from certain MM-info-record as sales price
entry.content:
Dear gurus,

 

we would like to use the purchase price of a material as the sales price. We do NOT use IS Retail. Details:

  • We would like to use condition type PR01.
  • We would like to have the system add that condition type automatically in sales order item in VA01.
  • We would like to find the correct sales price by looking up the default info-record for the material from the source list. Then we would like to use the purchase price maintained in that info record.

Questions:

  • There is no standard way to do this, correct?
  • We think we need to create a formular for condition value in VOFM. Correct?
  • Does anyone have any example code on this?
  • But that will not insert PR01 in VA01 automatically, correct?

Thanks
Jens

entry.title: Re: GL clear
entry.content:
Well, I could not understand the question completely nonetheless let me try to answer.

You have posted in special period to a G/L account managed on OI basis and you want to clear this using F-53. If this is the case, then you should use F-03. F-53 will give you only vendor OIs and not G/L OIs.

entry.title: Re: SAP CodeJam in Warsaw: Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core
entry.content: We are looking forward to it! Here is the event to register: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-codejam/getting-started-with-generative-ai-hub-on-sap-ai-core/ev-p/13809704

entry.title: Planned Order Process-( PP -EWM Integration)
entry.content:
Hallo All,

I am testing Planned order Process. When i do the MRP Run the system has created a Planned order. I had also maintained control cycle for Components (3000090,3000091, 3000092)  of Main material(1000050).  In the control cycle i maintained Staging Indicator as 3 (Release Order Parts).  i had followed the steps mentioned in the following blog.
https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/production-staging-using-ewm-ppmf60-sandeep-koyya/
 My Production and AFS storage location are EWM managed in a single Warehouse. 

While Executing MF60 when i click on stage the status of material is not changed to green and  the save option is not available as mentioned in the blog. There is no outbound delivery is created. i had checked in SLG1 for any log error, but no logs are created.
I do not understand what i am missing. I request you please guide me to solve this issue. 

i really appreciate you help if you provide detailed configuration guide and process to execute Planned Order in PP-EWM integration.

Thank you,

Regards,
Venu

However, when the midColumnPage is populated by clicking a row the settings button goes into overflow. I can control other custom controls' overflow behaviour but not the settings button that smarttable gives by default. I don't need the Settings button to go into overflow.

entry.title: Post debit to specific G/L account upon goods receipt
entry.content:
Hi experts,
In the PO pricing procedure I'm using a condition type (ZCOS) to record a surcharge cost. As this cost might or not occur, the condition type is set as "Manual", "Condition Category" is empty and it does not have an "Access Sequence".
The surcharge cost must be posted in a specific G/L cost account (712001).

Imagine the following scenario:
- PO where we are buying 10 units of material XPTO ( PBSG 10 EUR);
- Surcharge cost (ZCOS) of 5 EUR it's added to the PO

My goal is to have the following postings when doing the goods receipt of the PO:
DEBIT - Inventory: 100 EUR
CREDIT - GR/IR: 105 EUR
DEBIT - Surcharge account 712001: 5 EUR

As you can see, I expect the surcharge cost to be credited in GR/IR and debited in our G/L account 712001.

Unfortunatelly, until now I was not able to get it and I tried different configurations like:

# Configuration 1 #
Condition Type ZCOS | Cond.Class: A, Calc.Type: B, Cond.Categ.: "", Accruals checkbox not ticked
Pricing Procedure | ZCOS with "Statistical" not ticked, having an "Account Key" that in OBYC points to 712001, "Accrual Key" empty

# Result of Config.1 #
DEBIT - Inventory: 100 EUR
CREDIT - GR/IR: 105 EUR
DEBIT - Purch.Price Variance: 5 EUR

As you can see the debit of the surcharge cost went to Price Variance account instead of our account 712001

# Configuration 2 #
Same as configuration 1 with only one difference, Condition Type ZCOS with Cond.Category "B" (delivery costs)

# Result of Config.2 #
Exactly the same as Config.1

# Configuration 3 #
Condition Type ZCOS | Cond.Class: A, Calc.Type: B, Cond.Categ.: "", Accruals ticked
Pricing Procedure | ZCOS with "Statistical" not ticked, having an "Accrual Key" that in OBYC points to 712001

# Result of Config.3 #
DEBIT - Inventory: 100 EUR
CREDIT - GR/IR: 100 EUR
DEBIT - Purch.Price Variance: 5 EUR
CREDIT - Surcharge 712001: 5 EUR

With this I now have 712001 being posted but as a CREDIT and I want a DEBIT. Also GR/IR is not being impacted.
We need to see something like this:
DEBIT - Inventory: 100 EUR
CREDIT - GR/IR: 105 EUR
DEBIT - Surcharge account 712001: 5 EUR

Can you help me on this?
Thanks

Can you please suggest if we are missing something.

 

Regards,

Abhishek

entry.title: Re: SAC Report Broadcasting - Blog 1 - Imported Data

entry.title: Supplier payment memo line rules
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

We are uploading the bank statement file (inbound file) in SAP Business ByDesign. but in this file, the banker is not given the vendor invoice reference ID. They provided only the vendor name. How to define vendor payment memo line rules?

The vendor payments are done with Direct Debit.

Please guide me on how to define supplier payment memo line rules with all types of payments and without reference ID.

Thanks & Regards,

DNRAO

entry.title: Custom Field in Master Form
entry.content:
I´m trying to extract a field that is not available in the form. Therefore, I created a cds + custom field and then a custom logic. Well, I´m able to create the custom field in the content form, but is possible to create the custom field in the master form? 

Thanks.

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Extensibility SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

entry.title: Re: How can I prevent the "delete" key from being deleted from the alv?
entry.content: It's been already answered in the forum. NO_ROWINS to prevent row insertion, copy and deletion.

entry.title: Re: SAC Report Broadcasting - Blog 1 - Imported Data
entry.content:
Hi @Mandar_Damle,

Thank you very much for your question.

I completely agree with you that it is a very basic requirement. From my research, I would also agree with you that it is, unfortunately, not currently possible. 

Please my second blog regarding SAC scheduling broadcast reports for live connections

I have discussed the compatible live data connections.

I hope it helps 🙂

entry.title: Re: do I need a test double for 'me' method call in method under test?
entry.content: Just test what is really worth testing, manual tests versus time spent on writing the ABAP Unit. No interest here, I guess.

entry.title: Re: Password file required for commandline job execution?
entry.content:
I am setting up SAP Data Services 4.2 SP14 Patch 27 on RedHat, and I have the same issue.

Looking at the exported commands, when you don't select use password file the shell script generated doesn't escape the double quotes used around some of the flags, -Q, -G, -ServerGroup in my case. 

Looks like a bug to me. One that still hasn't been fixed in a decade, but since "use password file" is the default, I can't say I'm surprised.

entry.title: Re: Manual DIstribution Rule mapping in SAP Business One...
entry.content:
Hi SOORAJ-CCS,

Unfortunately, the table OMDR for manual distribution rule is not exposed in DI API or Service Layer.

Therefore, you cannot create.

Kind regards,

ANKIT CHAUHAN

SAP Business One Support

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content:
Hi community members! It is great to meet and discuss in this group.

I´m located in Germany and work as a SAP Fieldglass Consultant. My Focus is on the E2E Process from the analysis till the go live of a SAP Fieldglass implementation project.

entry.title: Re: How to identify the exact SQL query sent to source system?
entry.content:
I have not found the answer to my question, but I have found the bug that prompted it

In this particular case I am reading from a remote Google Big Query source.
Parameterised query parameters are not logged there either because they might contain sensitive information.
So I have not been able to find out what exactly is being sent in the parameter.

But after testing, I have found the error.
There is a difference between GBQ and DSP data for the TIMESTAMP field.
In the GBQ table I have ‘2024-06-12 10:44:06.350283 UTC’ (read using GBQ console) and that same data read from the DSP remote table gives ‘2024-06-12 10:44:06.350282’. Note the difference in the last decimal place.

This makes the system inconsistent (data is not a exact COPY, and DIFFERS in the source system and in DSP) so queries do not return the expected data.

I will have to open another incident in SAP regarding GBQ integration.

entry.title: Re: How to identify the exact SQL query sent to source system?
entry.content: Thanks @Yogesh_Rehadwa , but in the monitor the parameterised queries are shown as I described, with ? and not the value.

entry.title: Re: How to schedule a daily shutdown for your Steampunk system?
entry.content: Thanks. I find the labels "from" and "to" meaningless because the dialog title is "schedule stop/start", we don't know if it's "stop from/stop to" or "start from/start to". Instead, respectively "stop at" and "start at" would be more clear. If by chance you can tell the developer team, thank you.

entry.title: Re: Integration Between SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud - Blog 4
entry.content:
Hi @sinhaanu,

Thank you for your question.

From my own research, at the moment there is no way planning functionality will work with an SAC Datasphere live connection.

As per the SAP Road Map Explorer, seamless planning integration with Datasphere is planned for Q3 2024. See Below.

 

I hope this helps and best of luck 🙂

kenji

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere - public vs private tenants
entry.content: As far as I know, there is no such option for DSP.

entry.title: Re: How to schedule a daily shutdown for your Steampunk system?
entry.content:
The problem is that the scheduling as such is not easy to understand and to use using the Landscape Portal app. 

For certain technical reasons one can only define time slots where a system is not available. 

If you want a change, e.g. a more easy to understand dialogue, please feel free to create a customer influence request.

entry.title: Re: Product Compliance in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408
entry.content:
Hello,

As mentioned above, this blog is not the right platform to discuss implementation issues for the on-premise version.
This blog is about the latest release of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and shows the latest innovations related to this release.
For questions regarding version SAP S/4HANA to get proper support for your more technical questions, we recommend directly leveraging our “community.sap.com” or here:
https://pages.community.sap.com/topics/product-compliance-applications

Best Regards

Christian

entry.title: Re: Exclude Condition Record by Item Category
entry.content: Can you please type the correct syntax

entry.title: Re: Doing cool stuff really easily with SuccessFactors Integration Centre
entry.content: To be honest, i didn't try the mentioned configurations in our system. The blog is just awesome.

entry.title: Entering batch at the time of Putaway - EWM
entry.content:
Hi All,

I need your help for following issue :

1) Product is batch managed

2) Inbound delivery is created in S4 & distributed to EWM. Inbound delivery is not having batch information.

3) As product is batch managed without providing the batch number EWM does not allow to do any further transaction

4) Users does not know the batch as Truck/van are outside & vendor has not provided any inputs

5) Why system is asking to put the Batch at the time of GR - ROD ? 

6) We can create DUMMY batch so that unloading can happen. There is an option further at the time of PUTAWAY task confirmation we can change the batch using exception code CHBA.

Ask is "system should ask for batch at the time of putaway". We have ROD and AFS concept and we are sending GR immediately.

Regards

Ashish

entry.title: Re: Upload Excel using SAP RAP Only
entry.content: I am facing an issue while uploading file using this method, When we choose the file in the popup in first go, it again opens the popup and second time when we choose the file then it takes the file. Problem is user has to perform two clicks to upload one file and has to choose the file from the popup twice. Can you suggest if this standard behaviour and if any fix or SAP Note available for this

entry.title: Master Page Data using Gateway Service in Adobe
entry.content: I'm developing an Adobe Form using a Gateway Service. What is the recommended way to add the Master Page Header (Logo) and Footer to the Form, since these are generally not part of the OData? Is it necessary to copy the Gateway Service?
